I'Iay l-8, 199 0
Mr. John Rothman
Legal Division
Fair Political, Practices Couoissiou
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 9 5814
Dear Mr. Rothman:
Woul-d you please confirm, in writing, your statement to me
by telephone on May 9, L99o that I have no confl-ict as a Richmond
planning comrnissioner in voting on a project that involves rny
wifers stepfather?
As you may recall-, ny wifets stepfather and I have no
financial connections of any kind. If you could please expedite
your repLy, as you mentioned, I vould be most grateful,.
Very truly yours,
,%-'7+
steve uarvS{


San Juan Capistrano
C,ommunity
Redevelopment
Agency
Jur g S sr lll 'S
29, 1990
Ms. Susan Bobrow
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street - Suite 100
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807
Re:
Letter No. 90-377/Request for Additional Information
Dear Ms. Bobrow:
This letter is provided to you at the request of City Attorney John R. Shaw-. _ Mr.
Shaw relates t'hat you need additional information which explains the scope of legal
services under a consulting agreement between the Community Redevelopment
Agency of the City of San Juan Capistrano and Consultant Nancy Erickson.
Section I of Mrs. Erickson's agreement provides that all work performed by her shall
be subject to review and approval by myself, acting as Community Redevelopment
Agency Executive Director. The full time employee staffing of the Redevelopment
A[ency is as follows: I am the Executive Director of the Agency, Jeff Parker is my
asiistant and Cassandra Walker is the Community Redevelopment Agency Manager.
Since Mrs. Erickson became a consultant to the Agency, she reports directly to Mr.
Parker and Ms. Walker. Her services are reviewed by these two individuals. They in
turn provide an update regarding the status of Agency projects to me. I give specific
direction regarding communications and negotiations carried on between the Agency
and project redevelopers. All Redevelopment Agency Redeveloper atreements are
ultimately approved or rejected by the Redevelopment Agency Board.
You have asked for additional information regarding items 3-A and 3-F under Exhibit
A to the Consulting Agreement. The use of the word "negotiaterr under scope of
services means that Mrs. Erickson is authorized to take specific direction from
Redevelopment Agency employees, including myself, and convey that direction to a
given project redeveloper. Information from such meetings is then relayed to my
Agency staff for independent review which is then reviewed by myself.
32.100 Paseo Adelanto
San Juan (.apisrrano
C;rlifornia 92675
7tl -493-t17t


June 29, 1990
Page 2
To take a case in point, the Redevelopment Agency consummated a lease
arrangement with a restaurateur who will be opening a restaurant within real
property owned by the Redevelopment ASency. (This particular project has nothing
to do with the Franciscan Plaza II which is discussed Mr. Shaw's letter) Mrs.
Erickson was involved in several discussions with the restaurateur. That process
involved direction being given by my subordinates and myself to Mrs. Erickson which
was then relayed vis a vis Mrs. Erickson to the restaurateur. Mrs, Erickson had no
authority to finalize deal points. To the extent that she had any advice or
recommendation on developing the lease agreement, her views were substantively
reviewed by my Agency subordinate as well as myself. The lease agreement was
eventually reviewed and approved per the final decision-making body, the
Redevelopment Agency.
With respect to Exhibit A, Item 3F, Mrs. Erickson has not attended any City Council
meetinSs, Planning Commission meetings, Redevelopment Agency meetinSs' or
Architectural Review Board meetings to date. Section 3-F was intended to deal
mainly with Mrs. Erickson's possible attendance at Redevelopment Agency meetings
where she would be available to answer factual questions.
Please be also advised that this is to confirm that Mrs. Erickson did not perform any
consulting services with respect to Phase II of the Franciscan Plaza pro,iect as noted
in Mr. Shawfs letter ol May 22, 1990.
I have personal knowledge of the above stated facts and am competent to testify as
to the truth of these facts if called as a witness.
If you should need any additional information, please feel lree to call this office.
Stephen B. Julian
Executive Director
SBJ:ms
cc: J. Shaw


FP' )
::
T,iEMBERS OF THE CiTY COUNCIL
I '_.'i 
aNTHoNy L. BLANo
:J 
LAwRENcE F. EUcHHEtM
KENNEIH E. FRIESS
GAFY L. HAUS OOhFER
PHILLIP R. SCH WARIZE
STEPXEN B.IULIAN
ivlay 22, 1990
Ms. Katherine Donovan, General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street - Sixth Floor
Sacramento, CA 9r814
Re:
Request Ior Inf ormal Opinion
Dear Ms. Donovan:
On behall of San Juan Capistrano Planning Commissioner, James Erickson, this letter
is to request an informal written opinion pursuant to Title 2, California Adminis-
trative Code Section 18329(cX2). The specific questions are whether Planning
Commissioner Erickson would:
Have a conflict oI interest under Government Code Section
87100 had he voted on a project known as Franciscan Plaza,
Phase II; and
Necessarily be disqualilied from voting on land use applications
before the Planning Commission involving proposed proiects
located within the redevelopment proiect area where:
i) 
His wife, Nancy Erickson, has performed consulting
work of the nature described below for the
Redevelopment Agency of the City of San Juan
Capistrano relating to projects which are later
reviewed by the City Planning Commission Ior
planning and zoning and other related land use
entitlements;
ii) 
His wife has not performed any consulting work
relating to a proiect in the redevelopment area
which later is reviewed by the Planning Commission
for planning and zoning entitlements,
a)
b)
324OO PASEO ADELANTO, SAN JUAN CAPISTRANO, CALIFORNIA 92675 ' (714) 49,3.II7I


Ms. Katherine Donovan
May 22, l99O
Page Two
1.
2.
FACTS
Planning Commissioner lames Erickson was appointed to the San Juan
Capistrano Planning Commission on November 21, 1989.
In April 1990, an Architectural Approval Application and Zone Variance
Request for a commercial retail project came before the Planning Com-
mission lor approval. This project is known as Franciscan Plaza, Phase II.
The project is located within the redevelopment project area formed by
the rrCommunity Redevelopment Agency of the City of San Juan
Capistranorr. The Redevelopment Agency is a separate governmental entity
existing under Health and Safety Code Sections 33000, et seq.
Planning Commissioner Erickson did not vote on this proiect in that he was
out of town on business. He did, however, wish to vote had he been able to
attend this Planning Commission meeting. The Planning Commission did
vote on the matter and the land use approvals are now pending before the
City Council,
Planning Commissioner Ericksonrs wife, Mrs. Nancy Erickson, was a full
time employee of the City of San Juan Capistrano through Suly 31, 1989.
Her job position was Community Development Administrator servicinS the
Redevelopment Agency ol the City of San Juan Capistrano. Her job duties
included, but were not limited to, day to day management and adminis-
tration responsibilities for redevelopment proiects within the
redevelopment area. During her employment tenure, prior to July 31,
1989, Mrs. Erickson assisted the Redevelopment Agency in consummating
a development and disposition agreement between the Redevelopment
Agency and the developer of Franciscan Plaza, Phase I. The development
and disposition agreement provided for the construction of a parking
facility on the proiect site, that is the Phase I portion of the proiect. The
Agency, under the atreement, would assist in financing a portion of the
structure in return for which the Agency would receive rights to use a
portion of the parking facility. Mrs. Erickson did not perform any work as
an employee with respect to any aspect of Phase II of the Franciscan Plaza
project,
After leaving employment with the City, Mrs. Erickson entered into an
independent contractor atreement with the Redevelopment Agency on
August l, 1989. (see enclosure) Under the agreement, she is td provide
advice and information to the Redevelopment Agency. She has no d'ecision
making authority. Neither she, nor Commissioner Erickson, have any say
in determining the extent of work to be performed under the independeni
contractor agreement. Further, this independent contractor agreement is
not in any way reviewed by the City planning Commission. Hei role under
the agreement is .to provide analysis, advlce, etc. to Redevelopment
Agency stafl on an rron call" basis.
4.
5.


Ms. Katherine Donovan
May 22, 1990
Page Three
6. 
Since entering into the independent contractor agreement, Mrs. Erickson
has submitted bi.llings for work performed relating to certain portions oI
Phase I of the Franciscan Plaza. The Franciscan Plaza project consists to
date of two phases with respect to required planning entitlements. Phase I
consisted oI an architectural approval and site plan layout of retail space,
a theatre, and the parking structure. Phase I was approved by the Planning
Commission prior to Mr. Ericksonrs appointment to the Planning
commission. Mrs. Ericksonrs billings are based on work performed in
providing background information to new Redevelopment Agency
employees to rrbring them up to speedrr with respect to the development
and disposition agreement between Agency and the developer and these
particular billings are limited solely to the Phase I portion of the project.
The above billings occurred between September 1989 to the present time.
The total billings submitted by Mrs. Erickson to the Atency involving her
Phase I consultation and a host of other tasks perlormed unrelated to the
Franciscan Plaza project now amount to more than Sl1000, but less than
S2o,ooo.
7. 
The architectural application and zone variance application belore the
Planning Commission are land use entitlement applications separate and
distinct lrom the Redevelopment Agency agreement, The Planning Com-
mission does not review or act upon any Redevelopment Agency
agreements. Conversely, the Redevelopment Agency does not in any way
act upon the planning and zoning entitlements discussed herein.
8. 
Funds paid by the Agency to Mrs. Erickson are solely Agency funds. The
Franciscan Plaza developer has not in any way compensated Mrs, Erickson
or the Agency lor Mrs. Erickson's consulting work.
Commissioner Erickson respectfully requests an informal written opinion as to the
above stated questions.
""$i.<U"r-
City Attorney
IRS/ef

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Planning Commissioner James Erickson would have a conflict of interest under Government Code Section 87100 regarding voting on the Franciscan Plaza Phase II project, given that his wife performs consulting work for the Redevelopment Agency that oversees projects reviewed by the Planning Commission.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: This document is a compilation of three separate 1990 letters: two incoming requests to FPPC and one agency response letter. The primary substantive matter is the May 22, 1990 request for informal opinion (Question/Facts format) regarding Commissioner Erickson's conflict of interest. The June 29 letter is a response providing additional factual information. The Harvey letter is a brief incoming request. No FPPC response/advice letter is included in this document, so conclusion sections are null.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Whether Planning Commissioner James Erickson would have a conflict of interest under Government Code Section 87100 regarding voting on the Franciscan Plaza Phase II project, given that his wife performs consulting work for the Redevelopment Agency that oversees projects reviewed by the Planning Commission.

embedding.summary: This is a compilation of three documents from 1990 requesting FPPC guidance: (1) Steve Harvey seeking confirmation of no conflict in voting on a planning matter involving his wife's stepfather; (2) San Juan Capistrano Redevelopment Agency providing additional information about a consulting agreement; and (3) City Attorney requesting an informal opinion on whether Planning Commissioner James Erickson has a conflict regarding voting on Franciscan Plaza Phase II, given his wife's consulting work with the Redevelopment Agency.
